title: A Study to Investigate the Safety, Pharmacokinetics, and Preliminary Effectiveness of GSK4418959 Alone or in Combination With Other Anti-cancer Agents in Participants With Solid Tumors

eligibility.text: Has a histologically diagnosed advanced (unresectable, metastatic or recurrent) solid tumor
eligibility.justification: This criterion protects patients from harm by ensuring they have a confirmed diagnosis of an advanced solid tumor.

eligibility.text: Has a known dMMR/MSI-H status as determined by a certified local laboratory at the time of Pre-screening or has an unknown Mismatch repair (MMR)/ Microsatellite Instability (MSI) status at the time of Pre-screening and MMR/MSI status will be determined by central reference laboratory
eligibility.justification: This criterion ensures a homogenous population for comparison, maximizing the likelihood of detecting treatment effects.

eligibility.text: Provides an archival or fresh (preferred) formalin fixed, paraffin embedded (FFPE) sample
eligibility.justification: This criterion ensures adequate tissue samples for analysis and diagnosis.

eligibility.text: Intends to receive GSK4418959 (alone or in combination with PD-1 inhibitor, as determined between Investigator and sponsor) as next line of treatment
eligibility.justification: This criterion ensures the patient's treatment plan aligns with the study protocol.

eligibility.text: Has an Eastern Cooperative Oncology Group (ECOG) performance status of 0-2
eligibility.justification: This criterion ensures patients are physically capable of participating in the trial and managing potential side effects.

eligibility.text: Is expected to have a minimum of 3 months life expectancy
eligibility.justification: This criterion ensures patient survival is sufficient for the study duration.

eligibility.text: Has adequate organ function, as defined in the protocol
eligibility.justification: This criterion ensures patients have sufficient organ function to tolerate treatment and participate in the trial.

eligibility.text: • Has histologically diagnosed advanced (unresectable, metastatic or recurrent) solid tumor and has exhausted all standard of care treatment options
eligibility.justification: This criterion ensures patients have exhausted other treatment options before participating in the trial.

eligibility.text: Has histologically diagnosed advanced (unresectable, metastatic or recurrent) Colorectal cancer (CRC) or Endometrial cancer (EC)
eligibility.justification: This criterion ensures a homogenous population for comparison and maximizes the likelihood of detecting treatment effects.

eligibility.text: Has received at least 1 but no more than 3 lines of systemic anticancer therapy for their advanced (unresectable, metastatic or recurrent) disease including at least one line of Immune checkpoint inhibitors (ICI) therapy
eligibility.justification: This criterion ensures patients have received a sufficient number of treatment lines to allow for comparison with the study drug.

eligibility.text: Has measurable disease (i.e., at least 1 target lesion) during the Screening period per RECIST 1.1, as determined by the investigator
eligibility.justification: This criterion ensures patients have a measurable disease to allow for comparison with the study drug.

eligibility.text: Has not recovered (i.e., to Grade ≤1 or to baseline) from prior anticancer therapy-induced AEs
eligibility.justification: This criterion ensures patients have recovered sufficiently from previous treatment side effects before participating in the trial.

eligibility.text: Has received prior treatment with a WRN inhibitor
eligibility.justification: This criterion ensures patients have not received WRN inhibitors, which could confound the study results.

eligibility.text: Is unable to swallow and retain orally administered study treatment
eligibility.justification: This criterion ensures patient compliance with the study protocol.

eligibility.text: Has symptomatic uncontrolled brain or leptomeningeal metastases
eligibility.justification: This criterion ensures patients have a condition that could be detrimental to their health and safety.

eligibility.text: Has a known additional malignancy that progressed or required active treatment within the last 2 years because reoccurrence of another malignancy would confound interpretation by RECIST 1.1 criteria. Exceptions include basal cell carcinoma of the skin, squamous cell carcinoma of the skin that has undergone potentially curative therapy, or in situ cancer that is considered to be low risk for progression by the investigator
eligibility.justification: This criterion ensures patients have a condition that could be detrimental to their health and safety.

eligibility.text: Has any impairment of gastrointestinal function or gastrointestinal disease that may significantly alter the absorption of study drugs
eligibility.justification: This criterion ensures patients have a condition that could affect their ability to participate in the trial.

eligibility.text: Has severe liver fibrosis
eligibility.justification: This criterion protects patients from harm by ensuring they have a condition that could be detrimental to their health and safety.

eligibility.text: Has cirrhosis or current unstable liver or biliary disease
eligibility.justification: This criterion ensures patients have a condition that could be detrimental to their health and safety.

eligibility.text: Has known hypersensitivity to any of the study interventions or any of their excipients
eligibility.justification: This criterion ensures patients have a condition that could be detrimental to their health and safety.

eligibility.text: Has known WRN syndrome
eligibility.justification: This criterion protects patients from harm by excluding those with a known genetic disorder.

eligibility.text: Has an active autoimmune disease that has required systemic treatment in the past 2 years
eligibility.justification: This criterion ensures feasibility by limiting the study population to those with a history of active autoimmune disease requiring systemic treatment.

eligibility.text: Has experienced any of the following with prior immunotherapy: any immune mediated adverse events (imAE) of Grade ≥3, immune-related severe neurologic events of any grade, exfoliative dermatitis of any grade (Stevens-Johnson Syndrome, toxic epidermal necrolysis, or Drug rash with eosinophilia and systemic signs syndrome /[DRESS/] syndrome), or myocarditis of any grade.
eligibility.justification: This criterion prioritizes patient safety by excluding individuals who have experienced severe immune-related adverse events after prior immunotherapy.

eligibility.text: Has any history of interstitial lung disease or pneumonitis
eligibility.justification: This criterion protects patients from harm by excluding those with a history of interstitial lung disease or pneumonitis, which can be life-threatening.

power_analysis.assessment: N/A — Phase 1 trial, not powered for efficacy

randomization.randomization_description: Stratified by Baseline, Prior Treatment, Age.

adaptive_designs.description: Adaptive design detected: Types identified: Dose-Finding / Phase I.

safety_adverse_events.ae_ascertainment: Not explicitly described (assumed standard investigator reporting)
safety_adverse_events.safety_monitoring: Investigator-reported with sponsor oversight (no DSMB mentioned)

known_effects: Treatment response - WRN helicase inhibition in dMMR/MSI-H tumors

pharmacogenetics.genetic_screen_failure_summary: Detected 1 genetic biomarker requirement(s) in eligibility: dMMR_MSI_H (pop. prevalence ~15%, ~85% screen fail). Worst-case screen failure rate from genetic criteria: ~85%.
pharmacogenetics.summary: Genes detected (WRN) but could not map indication "solid tumors" to a GWAS EFO trait. Try alternative trait name.
